terms.title: General Terms and Conditions
terms.subtitle: These General Terms and Conditions (T&C) apply to all transactions with Mixel IT. Our products and services are exclusively aimed at customers with residence or company headquarters in Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
terms.section1.title: 1. Scope and Terms of Use
terms.section1.content: These T&C apply to all transactions concluded via www.mixel.ch and directly on-site. Prices include the applicable value-added tax. This offer is subject to the condition of availability and correct pricing. Mixel IT reserves the right to change these T&C and prices at any time. The version valid at the time of ordering shall apply.
terms.section2.title: 2. Contract Formation
terms.section2.content: The contract for products or services is concluded as soon as the customer places their order in the online shop, at a branch, by telephone or email. After conclusion of the contract, the customer receives an automatically generated order confirmation by email. This indicates that the order has been received and the contract has been concluded subject to availability and correct pricing.
terms.section3.title: 3. Delivery and Delivery Time
terms.section3.content: Delivery is made via A-Post exclusively to addresses in Switzerland or Liechtenstein. In case of delivery delay, the customer has the right to withdraw from the contract within 14 days from the order date. Mixel IT will refund amounts already paid. No further claims arise due to delivery delay.
terms.section4.title: 4. Payment
terms.section4.content: Unless otherwise agreed, payments must be made in Swiss Francs net within ten days of receipt of invoice. Various payment methods are available in the online shop: credit cards, PayPal, TWINT, PostFinance, advance payment and invoice with installment option (POWERPAY). For payment processing via third-party providers, their T&C apply additionally.
terms.section5.title: 5. Returns and Cancellation
terms.section5.content: Products can be returned within 14 calendar days of delivery. Full refund of the purchase price upon complete return. For individual inspection (functional defects, deviating signs of use), a deduction of at least 10% of the purchase price applies. Shipping costs for returns are borne by the sender, except for defective products (DOA).
terms.section6.title: 6. Warranty and Guarantee
terms.section6.content: The warranty period varies and is defined in the online shop and on the invoice. Mixel IT assumes the warranty for defect-freeness and functionality. The warranty can be provided through free repair, replacement with an equivalent product, credit or reduction. The warranty period for batteries and accessories is maximum 3 months. For repairs, the warranty is 3 months.
terms.section7.title: 7. Liability and Limitation of Liability
terms.section7.content: Liability is governed by the applicable legal provisions. Mixel IT is not liable for slight negligence, indirect and consequential damages, as well as damages from delivery delay. Liability is excluded for improper storage or use, use of incompatible spare parts, improper modification by third parties, and force majeure.
terms.section8.title: 8. Data Protection and Data Transfer
terms.section8.content: Mixel IT performs data deletions and resets during goods receipt and inspection, which lead to permanent deletion of existing data. The customer is obliged to backup personal data before sending. Mixel IT excludes any liability for data loss to the extent permitted by law. The privacy policy is an integral part of these T&C.
terms.section9.title: 9. Contract Duration and Termination
terms.section9.content: Service contracts are concluded for a fixed term of 24 months. After expiry, the contract is automatically extended for another 12 months unless terminated in writing with three months' notice. Termination must be made in text form (letter or email) to Mixel IT.
terms.section10.title: 10. Jurisdiction and Applicable Law
terms.section10.content: The legal relationships between Mixel IT and the customer are exclusively subject to Swiss law. Place of jurisdiction is Zug. Should individual provisions of these T&C be invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions shall remain unaffected.
terms.contact.title: Questions about our T&C?
terms.contact.content: If you have any questions about our General Terms and Conditions, please contact us:
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Your privacy is important to us. This privacy policy informs you about the nature, scope and purpose of the processing of personal data by Mixel IT.
privacy.section1.title: 1. Data Controller
privacy.section1.intro: Responsible for data processing on this website and contact for data protection questions is:
privacy.section2.title: 2. Principles of Data Processing
privacy.section2.content: We process personal data in accordance with the provisions of the Swiss Data Protection Act (DSG) and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We only collect and process personal data to the extent necessary for the provision of our services, compliance with legal obligations or based on your consent. Personal data is neither sold by us nor passed on to third parties for other reasons, unless this is necessary for the performance of the contract or we are legally obliged to do so.
privacy.section3.title: 3. Types of Data Collected
privacy.section3.intro: We collect and process the following categories of data:
privacy.section3.contact_desc: Name, email address, phone number, postal address (when you contact us or use our services)
privacy.section3.contract_desc: Information on ordered products/services, billing data, payment information
privacy.section3.technical_desc: IP address, browser type and version, operating system, referrer URL, hostname, time of server request
privacy.section4.title: 4. Purpose of Data Processing
privacy.section4.intro: We process your personal data for the following purposes:
privacy.section4.purpose1: Provision and operation of our IT services
privacy.section4.purpose2: Processing inquiries and communication with customers
privacy.section4.purpose3: Contract processing, invoicing and payment processing
privacy.section4.purpose4: Compliance with legal retention and documentation obligations
privacy.section4.purpose5: Improvement of our website and services
privacy.section5.title: 5. Legal Basis for Processing
privacy.section5.content: The processing of your personal data is based on Art. 6 Para. 1 of the Swiss DSG or Art. 6 GDPR: (a) Consent for specific processing purposes, (b) Contract performance or pre-contractual measures, (c) Compliance with legal obligations, (f) Legitimate interests for providing and improving our services.
privacy.section6.title: 6. Retention Period
privacy.section6.content: We store your personal data only as long as necessary for the fulfillment of the stated purposes or as long as legal retention periods exist. Contact inquiries are deleted after complete processing, unless a business relationship is established. Contract-related data is stored in accordance with commercial and tax retention periods (usually 10 years). Technical access data (log files) are automatically deleted after 90 days.
privacy.section7.title: 7. Disclosure to Third Parties and Service Providers
privacy.section7.intro: We only pass on your data to third parties if this is necessary for contract fulfillment, you have consented or we are legally obliged to do so. We use the following service providers:
privacy.section7.hosting_desc: Our website is hosted on servers in Switzerland or the EU. The hosting provider processes data exclusively on our behalf.
privacy.section7.analytics_desc: We do not use third-party tracking or analytics tools that collect personal data.
privacy.section7.email_desc: For email communication, we use secure Swiss or EU-based email servers.
privacy.section8.title: 8. Your Rights
privacy.section8.intro: You have the following rights regarding your personal data:
privacy.section8.right1_desc: You can request information about your personal data stored by us at any time.
privacy.section8.right2_desc: You have the right to have incorrect data corrected.
privacy.section8.right3_desc: You can request the deletion of your data, provided there are no legal retention obligations.
privacy.section8.right4_desc: You can request the restriction of the processing of your data.
privacy.section8.right5_desc: You have the right to receive your data in a structured, common format.
privacy.section8.right6_desc: You can object to the processing of your data for reasons arising from your particular situation.
privacy.section9.title: 9. Data Security
privacy.section9.content: We use technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against accidental or intentional manipulation, loss, destruction or access by unauthorized persons. These include: SSL/TLS encryption for data transmission, firewall systems, regular security updates, access restrictions and secure passwords. Our security measures are continuously improved in line with technological developments.
privacy.section10.title: 10. Cookies and Tracking
privacy.section10.content: Our website uses technically necessary cookies to ensure the functionality of the website (e.g. language settings, session management). These cookies do not store any personal data and are automatically deleted at the end of the browser session. We do not use tracking cookies or third-party analytics tools. You can deactivate cookies in your browser settings at any time, but this may limit the functionality of the website.
privacy.section11.title: 11. Changes to the Privacy Policy
privacy.section11.content: We reserve the right to adapt this privacy policy as necessary to bring it into line with changes in the legal situation or changes to our services and data processing. The current version is available on our website. In the event of significant changes, we will inform you by email if you have provided us with your contact details.
privacy.contact.title: Contact for Data Protection Questions
privacy.contact.content: If you have any questions about data protection, exercising your rights or complaints, please contact us:

home.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
home.faq.subtitle: Clear answers for Swiss SMEs evaluating IT partners, app projects and managed services.
home.faq.cta_text: Need a specific answer for your environment?
home.faq.item1.q: How quickly can Mixel take over our IT environment?
home.faq.item1.a: After a short assessment, onboarding usually starts within a few business days. Typical implementation windows range from 2 to 6 weeks depending on complexity.
home.faq.item2.q: Do you support existing systems or only new projects?
home.faq.item2.a: Both. We can stabilize existing infrastructure first and then modernize step-by-step without forcing a full replacement.
home.faq.item3.q: Can you build custom business apps for our workflows?
home.faq.item3.a: Yes. We build and operate custom apps, client portals, automation flows and AI features around your existing operations.
home.faq.item4.a: Support is coordinated by our Swiss team with clear responsibilities, SLA tracking and direct communication channels.
home.faq.item5.a: We support SMEs and growing enterprises across professional services, logistics, construction, finance and regulated operations.
home.faq.item6.q: How does access to restricted portals work?
home.faq.item6.a: Portal access is role-based and approval-driven. Only authorized accounts can enter systems like Accounting, Ticketing, Marketing, AI and KYC.
